metric: 0.1 System Startup and Main Menu Display
description: Execute the startup command. Observe whether the command line starts without error and displays a main menu with five clearly distinguishable top-level options: "Data Management," "Cost Calculation," "Combo Generation," "Combo Evaluation," and "Exit".
expected_output: Program starts normally and displays the main menu

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that all can be accessed from the main menu: "Data Management," "Cost Calculation," "Combo Generation," "Combo Evaluation." 2. Preparation: Enter the submenu of "Data Management," "Cost Calculation," "Combo Generation," and "Combo Evaluation" respectively. 3. Execution: Enter 'q' and press enter in each submenu interface. 4. Assertion: Verify that after each operation, the program accurately returns to the main menu rather than exiting the program or becoming unresponsive.
expected_output: Able to access each submenu and return to the main menu

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): After selecting "Data Management" from the main menu, confirm that there is a clearly distinguishable "Import Ingredient CSV" option in the menu. If inaccessible, this item scores 0 points. 2. Preparation: Create a file named `ingredients.csv` containing 5 rows of data that fully comply with the PRD standards. 3. Execution: Navigate to the "Import Ingredient CSV" feature and follow the prompts to enter the path of `ingredients.csv`. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the command line outputs a clear prompt of "Successfully imported 5 ingredient data entries."
expected_output: Prompts successful import of the specified number of ingredient data

metric: 2.1.1b Validation on Ingredient CSV Data Import Failure
description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): After selecting "Data Management" from the main menu, confirm that there is a clearly distinguishable "Import Ingredient CSV" option in the menu. If inaccessible, this item scores 0 points. 2. Preparation: Create a `bad_ingredients.csv` file where the "purchase unit price" column in the second row contains non-numeric text (e.g., "abc"). 3. Execution: Attempt to import `bad_ingredients.csv`. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the program fails to import and displays a Chinese error message containing keywords such as "line 2," "unit price," or "format error."
expected_output: Indicates import failure due to format error on a specific line of data

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): After selecting "Data Management" from the main menu, confirm that there is a clearly distinguishable "Manual Ingredient Entry" option in the menu. If inaccessible, this item scores 0 points. 2. Preparation: Prepare a set of complete and valid ingredient data (e.g., I100, Potato, Vegetable, kg, 5, 10). 3. Execution: Enter the "Manual Ingredient Entry" feature and follow the prompts to enter all the valid data prepared. 4. Assertion: Verify whether all the data can be successfully entered and receive a prompt of "Ingredient entry successful" in the last step.
expected_output: Prompts successful ingredient entry

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): After selecting "Data Management" from the main menu, confirm that there is a clearly distinguishable "Manual Ingredient Entry" option in the menu. If inaccessible, this item scores 0 points. 2. Preparation: Enter the "Manual Ingredient Entry" feature. 3. Execution: When prompted to enter "Processing Loss Rate (%)," input an invalid value, such as "50". 4. Assertion: Verify whether the system displays a Chinese error message (containing keywords like "between 0-30") and allows the user to re-enter the field.
expected_output: Indicates invalid input value and prompts for re-entry

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): After selecting "Data Management" from the main menu, confirm that there is a clearly distinguishable "Query Ingredient" option in the menu. If inaccessible, this item scores 0 points. 2. Preparation: Ensure that there is an ingredient named "Wuchang Rice" in the database. 3. Execution: Enter the "Query Ingredient" feature, choose to query by name, and enter "Wuchang Rice." 4. Assertion: Verify whether the query result correctly displays all information of "Wuchang Rice," such as unit price, loss rate, and stock quantity.
expected_output: The query result correctly displays the complete information of the specified ingredient

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): After selecting "Data Management" from the main menu, confirm that there is a clearly distinguishable "Query Ingredient" option in the menu. If inaccessible, this item scores 0 points. 2. Preparation: Ensure that there are at least 2 kinds of ingredients in the database categorized as "Staple Food." 3. Execution: Enter the "Query Ingredient" feature, choose to query by category, and enter "Staple Food." 4. Assertion: Verify whether the query result displays the information of all ingredients categorized as "Staple Food" in a list form.
expected_output: The query result displays all ingredients of the specified category in a list form

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): After selecting "Data Management" from the main menu, confirm that there is a clearly distinguishable "Manage Extra Costs" or similar option in the menu. 2. Preparation: Enter this feature and choose "Add". 3. Execution: Follow the prompts to enter the name and amount of the new cost item. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the system displays a "Added successfully" prompt. Also, verify whether this new cost item can be selected in the "Combo Cost Calculation" process.
expected_output: Prompts extra cost item added successfully

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): After selecting "Cost Calculation" from the main menu, confirm that there is a clearly distinguishable "Create New Combo" option in the menu. If inaccessible, this item scores 0 points. 2. Preparation: Assume the price of "Wuchang Rice" is 10 yuan/kg and the loss rate is 10%. 3. Execution: Enter "Create New Combo," add the ingredient "Wuchang Rice," and input "0.15" for the amount. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the system calculates the cost for this entry as 10 * 0.15 * (1 + 10/100) = 1.65 yuan.
expected_output: Unit test passes

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that the "Create New Combo" feature is available. 2. Preparation: In one combo, two ingredients have already been added with calculated costs of 1.65 yuan and 5.25 yuan, respectively. The packaging box cost is fixed at 1.5 yuan. 3. Execution: Complete the ingredient addition and enter the cost summary step. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the total cost displayed by the system sums up to 1.65 + 5.25 + 1.5 = 8.40 yuan.
expected_output: Unit test passes

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that the "Create New Combo" function is functional and total cost can be calculated. 2. Preparation: In a combo with a total cost of 8.40 yuan, disposable tableware costs 0.8 yuan. 3. Execution: Choose "y" for the extra cost item disposable tableware, and "n" for others. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the final total cost updates to 8.40 + 0.8 = 9.20 yuan.
expected_output: Correct total cost calculation

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that combo cost can be successfully calculated. 2. Preparation: Complete a combo cost calculation with at least 2 types of ingredients. 3. Execution: View the final output cost details report. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the report is a table with clear borders and headers (such as ingredients, amount, cost) with all data columns vertically aligned.
expected_output: Outputs a well-formatted cost details report

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): After selecting "Combo Generation" from the main menu, confirm that the feature can be accessed. If inaccessible, this item scores 0 points. 2. Preparation: Prepare a set of valid generation parameters (e.g., Single meal, 0.9 discount, 30% profit margin, high stock priority). 3. Execution: Enter the "Combo Generation" module and input all valid parameters sequentially. 4. Assertion: Verify whether all parameters can be successfully input and proceed to the subsequent combo generation stage, rather than encountering errors or becoming stuck.
expected_output: Parameters are successfully inputted and combo generation begins

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): After selecting "Combo Generation" from the main menu, confirm that the feature can be accessed. If inaccessible, this item scores 0 points. 2. Preparation: Enter the "Combo Generation" module. 3. Execution: When prompted to enter the "Discount Strength," input an invalid value "1.1". 4. Assertion: Verify whether the system displays a Chinese error prompt containing keywords like "between 0.1-1.0" and prompts the user to re-enter.
expected_output: Indicates invalid input value and prompts for re-entry

metric: 2.3.2 Core Combination Logic and Nutrition Constraints
description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that the "Combo Generation" feature can be accessed and parameters inputted. 2. Preparation: Ensure that the ingredient database contains sufficient supplies of staple foods, proteins, and vegetables. Request generation of a "Single Meal." 3. Execution: Observe the 3-5 candidate combos generated. 4. Assertion: Check the generated candidate combos, verifying whether each group strictly contains at least: 1 type of staple food (≥0.1kg), 1 type of protein (≥0.08kg), and 1 type of vegetable (≥0.1kg).
expected_output: Unit test passes

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that the combo can be successfully generated. 2. Preparation: Generate a combo and note its total cost. Set the profit margin to 30%. 3. Execution: View the suggested price of the combo. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the system's calculated basic price equals Cost * (1 + 0.3). A deviation of 0.01 is allowed.
test_command: cp evaluation/ingredients_for_test_2.3.2.csv src/data/ingredients.csv && python src/main.py < evaluation/inputs_for_test_2.3.1a.in
expected_output: Suggested price correctly calculated based on cost and profit margin

testcases.test_command: cp evaluation/ingredients_for_test_2.3.2.csv src/data/ingredients.csv && python src/main.py < evaluation/inputs_for_test_2.3.1a.in

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that the combo can be successfully generated and the basic price can be calculated. 2. Preparation: A single meal with a calculated basic price and a minimum price constraint of 28 yuan. 3. Execution: View the final displayed suggested price. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the final price satisfies the minimum price constraint (≥28) and is rounded properly.
test_command: cp evaluation/ingredients_for_test_2.3.2.csv src/data/ingredients.csv && python src/main.py < evaluation/inputs_for_test_2.3.1a.in
expected_output: Suggested price is properly constrained and rounded

testcases.test_command: cp evaluation/ingredients_for_test_2.3.2.csv src/data/ingredients.csv && python src/main.py < evaluation/inputs_for_test_2.3.1a.in

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that the candidate combo list can be successfully generated and displayed. 2. Preparation: Select a candidate combo, note its total cost C, selling price S, and discount D (e.g., 0.9). 3. Execution: View the profit margin displayed by the system. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the displayed profit margin equals (S*D - C) / C in calculation result (shown as a percentage, allowing a 0.1% error).
test_command: cp evaluation/ingredients_for_test_2.3.2.csv src/data/ingredients.csv && python src/main.py < evaluation/inputs_for_test_2.3.1a.in
expected_output: Correct profit margin calculation

testcases.test_command: cp evaluation/ingredients_for_test_2.3.2.csv src/data/ingredients.csv && python src/main.py < evaluation/inputs_for_test_2.3.1a.in

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that the candidate combo list can be successfully generated and displayed. 2. Preparation: Select a candidate combo that contains 4 different ingredients. 3. Execution: View the nutrition score displayed by the system. 4. Assertion: Verify whether its nutrition score equals 5 + (4 - 3) = 6 points.
expected_output: Correct nutrition score calculation

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that the candidate combo list can be successfully generated and displayed. 2. Preparation: Generate at least 3 candidate combos. 3. Execution: View the generated candidate combo list. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the list is strictly arranged in descending order of "profit margin".
expected_output: Results sorted by profit margin from high to low

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that the candidate combo list can be successfully generated and displayed. 2. Preparation: Generate candidate combos. 3. Execution: View the generated candidate combo list. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the entire list is displayed in a tabular form with borders and headers that include key data columns like combo ID, cost, price, profit margin, nutrition score, etc., and data alignment.
expected_output: Results displayed in a well-formatted table

description: 1. Pre-check (User Path): Confirm that the candidate combo list can be successfully generated and displayed. 2. Preparation: In the generated candidate combos list, there exists a combo with ID '1'. 3. Execution: Follow the prompt to input the combo ID '1' for saving. 4. Assertion: Verify whether the program gives a clear success prompt of saving, including an automatically generated combo name (like "Individual Nutrition Combo 1").
expected_output: Combo saved successfully with a displayed combo name
